statement.p: In still water a tugboat can travel 15\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut mi}{\rm\mathstrut hr}}. It travels 46\ {\rm mi} upstream and then 46\ {\rm mi} downstream in a total time of 6.19\ {\rm hr}. Find the speed of the current.

solution.p: Its given that the boat traveled at 15 miles per hour in still water. Assume the river flows at x miles per hour.
Then, the boat traveled upstream at 15-x miles per hour, and downstream at 15+x miles per hour.
Using the formula \text{time}=\frac{\text{distance}}{\text{speed}}, we know the boat traveled upstream for \frac{46}{15-x} hours; and it traveled downstream in \frac{46}{15+x} hours.
Its given that the whole trip took 6.19 hours, so we can write an equation and solve it:

In this situation, since speed cannot be negative, x={1.43519\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut mi}{\rm\mathstrut hr}}} is the only solution. The speed of the current was {1.43519\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut mi}{\rm\mathstrut hr}}} miles per hour.
